title: Reading Opponents & Tells
subtitle: Patterns, timing, and player types at the table
intro: Reading opponents starts with observing how they play hands — not guessing one tell in isolation. Bet sizing, frequency of raises and folds, and which boards they continue on reveal player type long before any physical gesture does.

Live poker adds physical and verbal tells: chip handling, eye contact, speech patterns, and reaction time. Online poker replaces much of that with timing tells, bet-size patterns, and stats like VPIP (voluntarily put money in pot) and PFR (preflop raise).

Strong readers combine multiple data points and adjust for context. A nervous shake might mean strength or weakness depending on the player. Use reads to refine strategy — bluff less vs calling stations, value bet thinner vs nits — but avoid over-weighting a single observation.

questions.question: The most reliable long-term read usually comes from:
options: Betting patterns and hand history over many hands
One physical gesture on the first hand

explanations.A: Correct — repeated actions reveal tendencies.
explanations.B: Single tells are noisy and unreliable alone.
explanations.C: Card back design isn't strategic info.
explanations.D: Seat number doesn't define play style.

remediation.question: Betting pattern tells include:
options: Always c-betting dry boards but checking wet ones

explanations.A: Correct — frequency and sizing patterns are reads.
explanations.C: Button is positional, not a tell by itself.

remediation.question: Tracking how often someone 3-bets preflop helps identify:

explanations.A: Correct — 3-bet frequency signals aggression.
explanations.D: Blind schedule isn't a player read.

questions.question: A 'timing tell' is:
options: How fast or slow someone acts — snap vs tank

explanations.A: Correct — decision speed can correlate with hand strength.
explanations.B: Wall clock isn't a player tell.
explanations.D: Dealer shuffle isn't player decision time.

remediation.question: Instant snap-call on river sometimes suggests:
options: Premeditated call — often strong but player-dependent

explanations.A: Correct — snap can mean trap or obvious call; know the player.

remediation.question: Long tank then large raise sometimes indicates:
options: Polarized decision — strong hand or elaborate bluff

explanations.A: Correct — tanks often mean a big decision point.

questions.question: Classic live physical tells include:
options: Shaking hands, eye contact changes, chip stacking behavior

explanations.A: Correct — body language and handling chips reveal stress or confidence.
explanations.B: Board order is public, not a player tell.

remediation.question: Reverse tells are:
options: Deliberately acting weak when strong or vice versa

explanations.A: Correct — skilled players fake tells to mislead.
explanations.D: Out-of-turn is angle, not reverse tell definition.

remediation.question: Physical tells are weakest when:
options: Sample size is one hand and player is unknown

explanations.A: Correct — one-off gestures mislead without context.

questions.question: A 'calling station' is a player who:
options: Calls too often — hard to bluff, good to value bet

explanations.A: Correct — stations call wide; reduce bluffs, increase value.
explanations.C: 3-bet aces only is nitty/ tight aggressive.
explanations.D: Stations see many flops by calling.

remediation.question: Vs a calling station you should:
options: Value bet thinner and bluff less

explanations.A: Correct — they pay off; extract value.

remediation.question: High VPIP and low fold-to-c-bet often signals:

explanations.A: Correct — loose and sticky postflop.
explanations.D: They hold cards fine — they call too much.

questions.question: A 'nit' is characterized by:
options: Very tight range — folds often, shows up with strong hands

explanations.A: Correct — nits enter pots selectively with premium holdings.
explanations.B: Loose aggressive is opposite of nit.

remediation.question: Vs a nit who suddenly raises river, you should:
options: Give credit — range is heavily value-weighted
Call with any high card always

explanations.A: Correct — nit river raises are rarely bluffs.
explanations.B: High card alone loses to nit value.

remediation.question: Exploit nits by:
options: Stealing blinds and folding to their aggression

explanations.A: Correct — they fold too much; respect their raises.
explanations.B: Light calls lose vs nit value.

questions.question: Online HUD stat PFR (preflop raise %) measures:
options: How often player raises preflop voluntarily

explanations.A: Correct — PFR tracks preflop raising frequency.
explanations.C: Showdown win rate differs from PFR.

remediation.question: VPIP measures:
options: Voluntarily put money in pot preflop — looseness indicator

explanations.A: Correct — high VPIP = plays many hands.
explanations.B: Blind defense can be voluntary but VPIP is broader.

remediation.question: High PFR + high VPIP suggests:

explanations.A: Correct — plays many hands and raises often.
explanations.D: High PFR means raising, not limping.

questions.question: Consistently betting 2x pot only with nuts and 1/3 pot with bluffs is:
options: A sizing tell — exploitable once identified

explanations.A: Correct — correlated sizing leaks information.
explanations.B: GTO mixes sizes with both value and bluffs.

remediation.question: When you spot sizing tells, exploit by:
options: Folding to big bets and calling small ones (if pattern holds)

explanations.A: Correct — adjust calls/folds to their size pattern.
explanations.B: Always calling burns chips vs value.

remediation.question: Min-bet on river from unknown player sometimes means:
options: Blocking bet or weak value — player-dependent

explanations.A: Correct — min-bets serve multiple purposes; gather more data.

questions.question: Speech play ('table talk') can be:
options: A deliberate tell or misdirection — use with caution
Always binding confession of hand strength

explanations.A: Correct — talk can reveal or deceive; rules vary by room.
explanations.C: Many rooms allow table talk with limits.
explanations.D: Live talk is common; online uses chat.

remediation.question: When opponent says 'I guess I'll call' and snaps chips:
options: Often strong — speech can feign weakness

explanations.A: Correct — weak-sounding speech + fast call can mean strength.

remediation.question: Best response to unknown speech play:
options: Weight it lightly until pattern confirmed

explanations.A: Correct — one line isn't enough data.

questions.question: You bet river, opponent tanks 30 seconds, sighs, and raises. Leveling means:
options: Thinking about what they think you have — can overcomplicate reads

explanations.A: Correct — leveling is meta-thinking that can lead to bad calls.

remediation.question: Vs unknown opponent, avoid leveling by:
options: Defaulting to population tendencies and pot odds first
Assuming every sigh is a hollywood bluff
Calling because they want you to fold

explanations.A: Correct — fundamentals beat fancy mind games early.

remediation.question: Hollywood (acting weak/strong) is:
options: Deliberate behavior to influence your decision

explanations.A: Correct — act can be sincere or fake.

questions.question: Player has 45% VPIP, 8% PFR, calls your c-bet 70% on dry boards, and min-raises river only twice (both nutted). You face river min-raise. Best adjustment:
options: Fold marginal bluff-catchers — sizing + stats show value-heavy line
Call every time — min-raise is always bluff

explanations.A: Correct — passive preflop + sticky postflop + nutted min-raises = fold weak catchers.
explanations.B: Their history shows value with this size.
explanations.D: Multiple data points form a strong read.

remediation.question: Combining reads means:
options: Stats + line + sizing together, not one tell alone

explanations.A: Correct — convergence strengthens decisions.

remediation.question: Passive preflop (low PFR) who raises river rarely suggests:
options: Strong made hand — they don't raise light often

explanations.A: Correct — passive players raise rivers with strength.
explanations.D: Raise is for value often here.

explanations.A: Correct.
Correct.

placementQuestions.question: Calling station:
options: Calls too much — value bet more

explanations.A: Correct.
Correct.
Correct.
Correct.

placementQuestions.question: Reverse tell:
options: Acting opposite of hand strength on purpose

explanations.A: Correct.
Correct.
